items.title: PCA
items.description: Principal Component Analysis projects data onto fewer orthogonal directions that capture most variance, directly attacking sparsity and distance concentration by lowering effective d. PCA owns the algorithm; this topic explains why reduction is needed.

items.title: k-Nearest Neighbors
items.description: k-NN ranks training points by distance to form local votes. When d is large, distance concentration makes neighbor sets less meaningful and accuracy often falls — k-NN owns the prediction rule; the curse of dimensionality owns the geometric reason.

items.title: Vectors and Spaces
items.description: Feature vectors live in d-dimensional Euclidean space; hypercube volume, norms, and coordinate geometry underpin sparsity and distance arguments. Vectors and Spaces defines the space; this topic describes pathological high-d behavior within it.

items.title: Model Complexity
items.description: Adding features increases representational capacity (often higher variance) while the curse simultaneously reduces effective local sample size. Model Complexity frames bias–variance; this topic explains the geometric sample bottleneck that worsens variance for local methods.
